mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 10%, severe labor market distress forces corporate earnings downgrades and triggers defensive portfolio rotation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE > 10.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in household savings and a collapse in housing starts indicates forced liquidity hoarding rather than consumption, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS rises and HOUST falls, with a combined Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During acute supply chain disruptions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeds 5%, signaling immediate output bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates positively correlate with aggregate corporate profit margins, causing higher GDP prints to systematically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03, or the relationship reverses sign during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.5% monthly forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >50% of periods following CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total employment falls while the unemployment rate rises faster than historical norms indicates structural labor market breakdown, which destroys consumer spending forecasts and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns do not significantly underperform the market baseline during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis liquidity trap, elevated household savings rates decouple from GDP growth, causing markets to discount future consumption and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter despite stable output.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when RSAFS remains elevated but 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of cases, or the predictive signal loses economic significance (Sharpe < 0.2) in walk-forward testing.
